era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt (Sixth Dynasty) continues under Pepi II. The Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin is at its territorial zenith. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase. This is the height of the Early Bronze Age III in the Levant and Anatolia.

events.title: Pepi II's reign continues; administrative consolidation in Egypt
events.description: Pepi II's long reign (traditional dating) continues with maintenance of Old Kingdom administrative structures. Inscriptions from officials' tombs (such as those of Harkhuf and Weni) document continued military and trading expeditions to Nubia, the Sinai, and the Red Sea coast. The power of provincial governors (nomarchs) is gradually increasing, foreshadowing the decentralization that will characterize the First Intermediate Period.
events.certainty_note: Pepi II's reign is traditionally dated c. 2278–2184 BCE. Specific events within his reign cannot be precisely dated to individual years.

events.title: Naram-Sin's military campaigns in the Zagros and Levant
events.description: Naram-Sin continues military campaigns to maintain Akkadian hegemony. Inscriptions record victories against the Lullubi and other highland peoples. The Akkadian Empire's control extends from the Persian Gulf to the Mediterranean and into Anatolia, though the stability of these conquests is increasingly questioned by modern scholars.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's campaigns are well-attested in contemporary inscriptions, but precise dating of individual campaigns is uncertain.

disconfirming_evidence: The extent of Akkadian control over distant regions remains debated. Some scholars argue that Akkadian 'empire' was more a network of trade relationships and tributary arrangements than direct territorial control. The claim that Akkadian influence reached the Indus Valley is based on limited archaeological evidence and is not universally accepted.
historiographic_note: Sources for this year are limited to royal inscriptions and administrative texts from Egypt and Mesopotamia. These are inherently biased toward elite narratives and military achievements. The vast majority of the world's population and civilizations are not documented in surviving sources.

graph_edges.note: Pepi II's reign spans multiple years; this entry documents the same reign as the previous year.
